recipes.title: Ugali
recipes.description: Kenya's staple stiff cornmeal porridge, eaten by hand alongside stews and vegetables.
steps: Bring the water to a rolling boil in a heavy-bottomed pot and add the salt.
Reduce the heat to medium and gradually rain in the maize flour with one hand while stirring constantly with a wooden spoon (mwiko) in the other.
Keep stirring vigorously to break up any lumps as the mixture thickens.
Once the dough pulls away from the sides of the pot, lower the heat and press it against the sides to cook the steam pockets out.
Cover and let it steam for 3-4 minutes, then turn and cook the other side for another 3 minutes.
Wet a round bowl, scoop the ugali into it, and invert onto a plate to form a smooth dome.
Serve hot, torn by hand, with sukuma wiki, nyama choma, or stews.

recipes.title: Sukuma Wiki
recipes.description: Quick-braised collard greens with onions and tomatoes, the everyday vegetable side of Kenyan tables.
steps: Wash the collard greens, stack the leaves, roll tightly, and slice into thin ribbons.
Heat the oil in a wide pan over medium heat and sauté the onion until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Add the garlic and ground coriander and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the chopped tomatoes and cook until they break down into a soft sauce, about 5 minutes.
Add the shredded collards a handful at a time, tossing to wilt before adding more.
Season with salt and pepper, splash in 60 ml water, cover, and steam for 5 minutes.
Uncover, toss to combine, and serve immediately alongside ugali.

recipes.title: Nyama Choma
recipes.description: Kenya's signature roasted goat meat, charcoal-grilled over open fire and served with kachumbari.
steps: Cut the goat into large bone-in pieces suitable for grilling.
Combine garlic, ginger, lemon juice, oil, salt, and pepper into a paste and rub all over the meat.
Marinate for at least 2 hours, ideally overnight, in the refrigerator.
Prepare a charcoal grill with the coals banked to one side for indirect heat.
Sear the meat over direct heat for 3-4 minutes per side to brown.
Move the pieces to the cooler side of the grill, cover loosely with foil, and slow-roast for 45-60 minutes, turning occasionally.
Brush with the leftover marinade halfway through cooking.
Rest the meat for 10 minutes, then chop into bite-sized pieces and serve with kachumbari and ugali.

recipes.title: Mukimo
recipes.description: Central Kenyan mash of potatoes, maize, beans, and pumpkin leaves from the Kikuyu community.
steps: Boil the potatoes and maize kernels together in salted water until the potatoes are fork-tender, about 20 minutes.
In a separate pan, heat the oil and sauté the onion until soft.
Add the pumpkin leaves and spinach and cook until fully wilted, about 4 minutes.
Drain most of the water from the potatoes, leaving just a little.
Add the cooked beans and the wilted greens to the potato pot.
Mash everything together with a wooden masher until the mixture is rustic and chunky, with the maize kernels still whole.
Season with salt, fold in the onion mixture, and serve warm alongside roasted meat or stew.

recipes.title: Kenyan Pilau
recipes.description: Coastal Swahili spiced rice cooked with beef, onions, and pilau masala for celebrations.
steps: Rinse the rice in cold water until it runs clear, then soak for 20 minutes and drain.
Heat the oil in a heavy pot and fry the sliced onions over medium heat until deeply caramelised, about 15 minutes.
Add the garlic, ginger, and pilau masala and cook for 1 minute until aromatic.
Stir in the beef and brown on all sides, about 8 minutes.
Add the tomatoes and potatoes, season with salt, and cook for 5 minutes.
Pour in the beef stock, cover, and simmer until the meat is tender, about 40 minutes.
Add the drained rice, stir gently once, and ensure the liquid sits 2 cm above the rice.
Cover tightly, lower the heat to minimum, and steam for 20 minutes without lifting the lid.
Rest off the heat for 10 minutes, then fluff with a fork and serve with kachumbari.

recipes.title: Mandazi
recipes.description: East African slightly sweet fried dough triangles flavored with cardamom and coconut milk.
steps: Whisk the flour, sugar, cardamom, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl.
In a separate bowl, beat the egg into the coconut milk.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and mix to a soft, slightly sticky dough.
Knead briefly until smooth, then cover and rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into four pieces. Roll each into a 1 cm thick circle and cut into 6 wedges.
Heat the oil in a deep pan to 170°C.
Fry the wedges in batches, turning once, for about 3 minutes total until puffed and golden.
Drain on paper towels and serve warm with chai.

recipes.title: Githeri
recipes.description: Traditional Kikuyu one-pot stew of boiled maize and beans, often enriched with vegetables and spices.
steps: Soak the maize and beans separately overnight in plenty of cold water.
Drain, combine in a large pot, cover with fresh water, and boil until very tender, about 90 minutes, topping up water as needed.
In a separate pan, heat the oil and sauté the onion until golden.
Add the garlic and ground coriander and cook briefly.
Stir in the tomatoes and cook into a soft sauce, about 6 minutes.
Add the carrot and potatoes and cook for 5 minutes.
Tip the cooked maize and beans (with about 250 ml of their cooking liquid) into the pan.
Season with salt and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes until the potatoes are tender and the stew thickens.
Taste, adjust salt, and serve hot.

recipes.title: Irio
recipes.description: Kikuyu green mash of potatoes, peas, and maize, traditionally served with grilled meats.
steps: Boil the potatoes in salted water until completely tender, about 20 minutes.
In a separate pot, simmer the peas and maize kernels together until soft, about 15 minutes, then drain.
Briefly blanch the spinach in boiling water for 1 minute and drain well.
Drain the potatoes and return to the pot.
Add the spinach and mash everything together until smooth and pale green.
Fold in the cooked peas and maize, keeping them mostly whole for texture.
Stir in the butter, season with salt, and serve warm next to nyama choma.

recipes.title: Chapati
recipes.description: Soft, layered Kenyan-style flatbread enriched and pan-fried with oil, served with stews.
steps: In a bowl, whisk the flour, salt, and sugar together.
Drizzle in 2 tablespoons of the oil and rub it into the flour with your fingertips.
Add the warm water gradually, mixing to form a soft dough.
Knead on a floured surface for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic, then rest covered for 30 minutes.
Divide into 8 balls. Roll one out into a thin disc, brush with oil, and roll up tightly into a rope.
Coil the rope into a snail shape, flatten, and rest while shaping the others.
Roll each coil into a 20 cm round disc.
Heat a flat skillet over medium-high heat. Cook each chapati for 1 minute per side, then brush both sides with oil and cook 30 seconds more per side until golden with brown spots.
Stack wrapped in a clean towel to keep soft and serve warm.

recipes.title: Maharagwe
recipes.description: Coastal Kenyan red kidney beans simmered in spiced coconut milk with tomatoes.
steps: Soak the beans overnight in plenty of cold water, then drain.
Boil the beans in fresh water until tender, about 60-70 minutes, and drain, reserving 200 ml of the cooking liquid.
In a wide pot, heat the oil and sauté the onion until soft and golden.
Add the garlic, ginger, turmeric, and cumin and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the tomatoes and cook until they break down, about 5 minutes.
Add the cooked beans and the reserved cooking liquid, then pour in the coconut milk.
Season with salt and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes until creamy and slightly thickened.
Taste, adjust salt, and serve with chapati or rice.

recipes.title: Mbuzi Choma
recipes.description: Charcoal-roasted goat ribs and leg, the centrepiece of Kenyan weekend gatherings.
steps: Pat the goat ribs dry and score the meat lightly between the bones.
Whisk garlic, ginger, lemon juice, oil, coriander, paprika, salt, and pepper into a paste.
Rub the paste over the meat and marinate for at least 4 hours, ideally overnight.
Light a charcoal grill and let it burn down to medium-low embers, with one side cooler than the other.
Sear the ribs over the hot side for 2-3 minutes per side to develop colour.
Move to the cooler side and roast slowly, turning every 10 minutes, for about 50 minutes until tender.
Brush occasionally with the leftover marinade thinned with a splash of oil.
Rest the ribs for 10 minutes, then chop between the bones and serve with kachumbari and ugali.

recipes.title: Kachumbari
recipes.description: Fresh East African salad of tomatoes, onions, chilli, and coriander, served with grilled meats.
steps: Slice the red onion very thinly and soak in cold water for 10 minutes to mellow its bite, then drain.
Combine the diced tomatoes, drained onion, and chilli in a serving bowl.
Drizzle with the lemon juice and season with salt and pepper.
Toss gently and let stand for 5 minutes for the flavours to mingle.
Fold in the chopped coriander just before serving.
Serve cold alongside nyama choma, pilau, or ugali.
